title: 'Viktor Orban's defeat in Hungary is truly a defeat for the reactionary international'
subhed: In an interview, Italian historian Steven Forti, a specialist in far-right movements, says that while Péter Magyar's victory is good news for the European Union, it remains unclear what alternative this conservative figure represents for the right – a question that resonates elsewhere.

title: French film star Nathalie Baye dead at 77
subhed: Baye, a stalwart of French cinema, starred in some 80 films and took home the Best Actress Cesar. She notably played Leonardo DiCaprio's mother in Steven Spielberg's 'Catch Me if You Can.'

title: Can we detox our bodies from pollutants like 'forever chemicals'?
subhed: Scientists are testing methods to remove certain pollutants from the human body. These methods can be both invasive and costly, and do not prevent renewed exposure to the most widespread environmental toxins.

title: How the tech lobby made secrecy part of EU law on data centers
subhed: Microsoft and the tech industry lobby secured a provision from the EU to keep environmental data about the massive data centers they operate in Europe confidential, according to an investigation by the Investigate Europe consortium, in collaboration with Le Monde.
